items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

source.title: Falls: assessment and prevention in older people and in people 50 and over at higher risk
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 years and over, and people aged 50 to 64 with one or more factors increasing fall risk.
source.setting: Community settings including emergency departments and minor injury units, and hospital inpatient settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for asking details of a fall and for comprehensive clinician assessment of specified falls-risk domains in the covered age group. It does not cover every traumatic injury, younger adults or children, and it must not auto-score fall risk.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of cardiovascular, cognition, delirium, diet/fluid, dizziness, footwear, function, gait/balance, hearing, long-term conditions, medicines, neurological, osteoporosis, continence and vision domains when relevant.

items.rationale: The exact section supports interval vision concerns as part of comprehensive falls assessment together with function, gait, cognition, medicines, hearing and support context; it does not diagnose eye disease.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting bereavement, living alone, limited social opportunity, separation, retirement, unemployment, low income, recent illness, driving cessation, age-related disability and advanced age as context without asserting loneliness or decline.

source.title: Older people: independence and mental wellbeing — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales public-health guidance; international evidence requiring UAE community and social-care adaptation
source.population: People aged 65 or older, with focus on those at risk of decline in independence and mental wellbeing.
source.setting: Community, public health, primary care, and services interacting with older people.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting social connection, living circumstances, life events, retirement, bereavement, driving cessation, age-related disability, loneliness, interests and support context. It does not generate an activity or service recommendation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.5.4 — identifying risk of decline in independence and wellbeing

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting bereavement, living alone, limited social opportunity, separation, retirement, unemployment, low income, recent illness, driving cessation, age-related disability and advanced age as context without asserting loneliness or decline.

items.rationale: The exact section supports documenting age-related vision impairment, driving cessation, living circumstances and social impact without generating ophthalmic management.
